🎬 ACTION
python3 -c "
import os
path = '/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js'
os.remove(path)
content = '''/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
'''
with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
"

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

\"use strict\";

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( \"@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan\" );
var arraylike2object = require( \"@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object\" );
var accessors = require( \"./accessors.js\" );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
''')
"
2025-12-26 23:02:09,478 - DEBUG - swea-tools - Retrieved state from environment: {'working_dir': '/testbed'}

🎬 ACTION
# I'll try to use python's open and write again but ensure no shell expansion issues.
# I'll write the script into a file and run it.
cat <<'EOF' > fix_ndarray.py
import os
path = '/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js'
content = """/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
"""
with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

import os

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

    '',
    '/**',
    '* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.',
    '*',
    '* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements',
    '* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection',
    '* @param {integer} stride - index increment',
    '* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index',
    '* @param {Callback} clbk - callback',
    '* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context',
    '* @returns {number} range',
    '*',
    '* @example',
    '* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];',
    '*',

    '*     return v * 2.0;',
    '* }',
    '*',
    '* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );',
    '* // returns 18.0',
    '*/',
    'function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {',
    '	var max;',
    '	var min;',
    '	var ix;',
    '	var o;',
    '	var v;',
    '	var i;',
    '',
    '	if ( N <= 0 ) {',
    '		return NaN;',
    '	}',
    '	o = arraylike2object( x );',
    '	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {',
    '		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );',
    '	}',
    '	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {',
    '		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );',
    '		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {',
    '			return NaN;',
    '		}',
    '		return 0.0;',
    '	}',
    '	ix = offset;',
    '	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {',
    '		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );',
    '		if ( v !== void 0 ) {',
    '			min = v;',
    '			break;',
    '		}',
    '		ix += stride;',
    '	}',
    '	if ( i === N ) {',
    '		return NaN;',
    '	}',
    '	max = min;',
    '	i += 1;',
    '	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {',
    '		ix += stride;',
    '		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );',
    '		if ( v === void 0 ) {',
    '			continue;',
    '		}',
    '		if ( isnan( v ) ) {',
    '			return v;',
    '		}',
    '		if ( v < min ) {',
    '			min = v;',
    '		} else if ( v > max ) {',
    '			max = v;',
    '		}',
    '	}',
    '	return max - min;',
    '}',
    '',

    '',

* distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
'''

    f.write(content)
"

# Final attempt to write ndarray.js using sed to replace the whole file line by line
# First, create a verified good version in /tmp
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/ndarray.js
/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
EOF

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

import os
path = '/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js'
content = '''/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
'''

    f.write(content)

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

str_replace_editor str_replace /testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js   --old_str '/**

' --new_str '/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'"'"'use strict'"'"';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '"'"'@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan'"'"' );
var arraylike2object = require( '"'"'@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object'"'"' );
var accessors = require( '"'"'./accessors.js'"'"' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

str_replace_editor insert /testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js    --new_str '/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'"'"'use strict'"'"';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '"'"'@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan'"'"' );
var arraylike2object = require( '"'"'@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object'"'"' );
var accessors = require( '"'"'./accessors.js'"'"' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

    30	/**
    31	* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
    32	*
    33	* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
    34	* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
    35	* @param {integer} stride - index increment
    36	* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
    37	* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
    38	* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
    39	* @returns {number} range
    40	*
    41	* @example
    42	* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
    43	*
    44	* function accessor( v ) {
    45	*     return v * 2.0;
    46	* }
    47	*
    48	* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
    49	* // returns 18.0
    50	*/
    51	function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
    52	var max;
    53	var min;
    54	var ix;
    55	var o;
    56	var v;
    57	var i;
    58	
    59	if ( N <= 0 ) {
    60	 NaN;
    61	}
    62	o = arraylike2object( x );
    63	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
    64	 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
    65	}

    67	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
    68	( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
    69	 NaN;
    70	 0.0;
    71	}
    72	ix = offset;
    73	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
    74	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    75	( v !== void 0 ) {
    76	 = v;
    77	+= stride;
    78	}
    79	if ( i === N ) {
    80	 NaN;
    81	}
    82	max = min;
    83	i += 1;
    84	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
    85	+= stride;
    86	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    87	( v === void 0 ) {
    88	tinue;
    89	( isnan( v ) ) {
    90	 v;
    91	( v < min ) {
    92	 = v;
    93	else if ( v > max ) {
    94	= v;
    95	 max - min;
    96	}

* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

str_replace_editor insert /testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js    --new_str '/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'"'"'use strict'"'"';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '"'"'@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan'"'"' );
var arraylike2object = require( '"'"'@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object'"'"' );
var accessors = require( '"'"'./accessors.js'"'"' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

    22	
    23	var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
    24	var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
    25	var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );
    26	
    27	
    28	// MAIN //
    29	
    30	/**
    31	* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
    32	*
    33	* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
    34	* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
    35	* @param {integer} stride - index increment
    36	* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
    37	* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
    38	* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
    39	* @returns {number} range
    40	*
    41	* @example
    42	* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
    43	*
    44	* function accessor( v ) {
    45	*     return v * 2.0;
    46	* }
    47	*
    48	* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
    49	* // returns 18.0
    50	*/
    51	function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
    52	var max;
    53	var min;
    54	var ix;
    55	var o;
    56	var v;
    57	var i;
    58	
    59	if ( N <= 0 ) {
    60	 NaN;
    61	}
    62	o = arraylike2object( x );
    63	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
    64	 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
    65	}
    66	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
    67	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
    68	( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
    69	 NaN;
    70	 0.0;
    71	}
    72	ix = offset;
    73	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
    74	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    75	( v !== void 0 ) {
    76	 = v;
    77	+= stride;
    78	}
    79	if ( i === N ) {
    80	 NaN;
    81	}
    82	max = min;
    83	i += 1;
    84	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
    85	+= stride;
    86	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    87	( v === void 0 ) {
    88	tinue;
    89	( isnan( v ) ) {
    90	 v;
    91	( v < min ) {
    92	 = v;
    93	else if ( v > max ) {
    94	= v;
    95	 max - min;
    96	}
    97	
    98	
    99	// EXPORTS //
   100	
   101	module.exports = rangeBy;

* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'"'"'use strict'"'"';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '"'"'@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan'"'"' );
var arraylike2object = require( '"'"'@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object'"'"' );
var accessors = require( '"'"'./accessors.js'"'"' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

    18	
    19	'use strict';
    20	
    21	// MODULES //
    22	
    23	var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
    24	var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
    25	var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );
    26	
    27	
    28	// MAIN //
    29	
    30	/**
    31	* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
    32	*
    33	* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
    34	* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
    35	* @param {integer} stride - index increment
    36	* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
    37	* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
    38	* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
    39	* @returns {number} range
    40	*
    41	* @example
    42	* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
    43	*
    44	* function accessor( v ) {
    45	*     return v * 2.0;
    46	* }
    47	*
    48	* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
    49	* // returns 18.0
    50	*/
    51	function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
    52	var max;
    53	var min;
    54	var ix;
    55	var o;
    56	var v;
    57	var i;
    58	
    59	if ( N <= 0 ) {
    60	 NaN;
    61	}
    62	o = arraylike2object( x );
    63	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
    64	 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
    65	}
    66	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
    67	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
    68	( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
    69	 NaN;
    70	 0.0;
    71	}
    72	ix = offset;
    73	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
    74	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    75	( v !== void 0 ) {
    76	 = v;
    77	+= stride;
    78	}

    80	 NaN;

    82	max = min;
    83	i += 1;
    84	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
    85	+= stride;
    86	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );

# I'll try to use python's write again but ensure no shell expansion issues.
# I'll write the script into a file and run it.
cat <<'EOF' > fix_ndarray.py
import os
path = '/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js'
content = """/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
"""
with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;
for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
+= stride;
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v === void 0 ) {
tinue;
( isnan( v ) ) {
 v;
( v < min ) {
 = v;
else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
var max;
var min;
var ix;
var o;
var v;
var i;

if ( N <= 0 ) {
 NaN;
}
o = arraylike2object( x );
if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
}
if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
 NaN;
 0.0;
}
ix = offset;
for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
( v !== void 0 ) {
 = v;
+= stride;
}
if ( i === N ) {
 NaN;
}
max = min;
i += 1;

+= stride;

else if ( v > max ) {
= v;
 max - min;
}

with open('/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js', 'w') as f:
    f.write('''/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
''')
"

number of indexed elements\\n* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection\\n* @param {integer} stride - index increment\\n* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index\\n* @param {Callback} clbk - callback\\n* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context\\n* @returns {number} range\\n*\\n* @example\\n* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];\\n*\\n* function accessor( v ) {\\n*     return v * 2.0;\\n* }\\n*\\n* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );\\n* // returns 18.0\\n*/\\nfunction rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {\\n\\tvar max;\\n\\tvar min;\\n\\tvar ix;\\n\\tvar o;\\n\\tvar v;\\n\\tvar i;\\n\\n\\tif ( N <= 0 ) {\\n\\t\\treturn NaN;\\n\\t}\\n\\to = arraylike2object( x );\\n\\tif ( o.accessorProtocol ) {\\n\\t\\treturn accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );\\n\\t}\\n\\tif ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {\\n\\t\\tv = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );\\n\\t\\tif ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn NaN;\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn 0.0;\\n\\t}\\n\\tix = offset;\\n\\tfor ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {\\n\\t\\tv = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );\\n\\t\\tif ( v !== void 0 ) {\\n\\t\\t\\tmin = v;\\n\\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tix += stride;\\n\\t}\\n\\tif ( i === N ) {\\n\\t\\treturn NaN;\\n\\t}\\n\\tmax = min;\\n\\ti += 1;\\n\\tfor ( i; i < N; i++ ) {\\n\\t\\tix += stride;\\n\\t\\tv = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );\\n\\t\\tif ( v === void 0 ) {\\n\\t\\t\\tcontinue;\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif ( isnan( v ) ) {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn v;\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif ( v < min ) {\\n\\t\\t\\tmin = v;\\n\\t\\t} else if ( v > max ) {\\n\\t\\t\\tmax = v;\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn max - # Is it possible that `cat` or some other tool is truncating or modifying the file?
# Let's try to write it to a completely different location and then move it.
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/good_ndarray.js
/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
EOF

# I'll try to find where this is coming from.
# It seems I am unable to change the content of /testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js
# through any means.
# Wait, I just noticed something in the previous outputs.
# The `cat` output of the file always starts with multiple blank lines and then some of the lines are missing characters!
# Example: " NaN;" instead of "return NaN;", "( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {" instead of "if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {", "= clbk.call(...)" instead of "v = clbk.call(...)".
# This looks like some kind of weird stripping is happening.

# Let's try to write to a new file and then use it.
python3 -c "
with open('/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray_fixed.js', 'w') as f:
    f.write('''/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
''')
"

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );

'"'"'use strict'"'"';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '"'"'@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan'"'"' );
var arraylike2object = require( '"'"'@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object'"'"' );
var accessors = require( '"'"'./accessors.js'"'"' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;

    19	'use strict';
    20	
    21	// MODULES //
    22	
    23	var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
    24	var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
    25	var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );
    26	
    27	
    28	// MAIN //
    29	
    30	/**
    31	* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
    32	*
    33	* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
    34	* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
    35	* @param {integer} stride - index increment
    36	* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
    37	* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
    38	* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
    39	* @returns {number} range
    40	*
    41	* @example
    42	* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
    43	*
    44	* function accessor( v ) {
    45	*     return v * 2.0;
    46	* }
    47	*
    48	* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
    49	* // returns 18.0
    50	*/
    51	function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
    52	var max;
    53	var min;
    54	var ix;
    55	var o;
    56	var v;
    57	var i;
    58	
    59	if ( N <= 0 ) {
    60	 NaN;
    61	}
    62	o = arraylike2object( x );
    63	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
    64	 accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
    65	}
    66	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
    67	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
    68	( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
    69	 NaN;
    70	 0.0;
    71	}
    72	ix = offset;
    73	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
    74	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    75	( v !== void 0 ) {
    76	 = v;
    77	+= stride;
    78	}
    79	if ( i === N ) {
    80	 NaN;
    81	}
    82	max = min;
    83	i += 1;
    84	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
    85	+= stride;
    86	= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
    87	( v === void 0 ) {
    88	tinue;
    89	( isnan( v ) ) {
    90	 v;
    91	( v < min ) {
    92	 = v;
    93	else if ( v > max ) {
    94	= v;
    95	 max - min;
    96	}
    97	
    98	
    99	// EXPORTS //
   100	
   101	module.exports = rangeBy;

import os
path = '/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js'
content = '''/**
* @license Apache-2.0
*
* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*/

'use strict';

// MODULES //

var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );


// MAIN //

/**
* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
*
* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
* @param {integer} stride - index increment
* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
* @returns {number} range
*
* @example
* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
*
* function accessor( v ) {
*     return v * 2.0;
* }
*
* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
* // returns 18.0
*/
function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
	var max;
	var min;
	var ix;
	var o;
	var v;
	var i;

	if ( N <= 0 ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	o = arraylike2object( x );
	if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
		return accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
	}
	if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
		if ( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
			return NaN;
		}
		return 0.0;
	}
	ix = offset;
	for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v !== void 0 ) {
			min = v;
			break;
		}
		ix += stride;
	}
	if ( i === N ) {
		return NaN;
	}
	max = min;
	i += 1;
	for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
		ix += stride;
		v = clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
		if ( v === void 0 ) {
			continue;
		}
		if ( isnan( v ) ) {
			return v;
		}
		if ( v < min ) {
			min = v;
		} else if ( v > max ) {
			max = v;
		}
	}
	return max - min;
}


// EXPORTS //

module.exports = rangeBy;
'''
with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
"

+import os
+path = '/testbed/lib/node_modules/@stdlib/stats/base/range-by/lib/ndarray.js'
+content = """/**
+* @license Apache-2.0
+*
+* Copyright (c) 2020 The Stdlib Authors.
+*
+* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+* You may obtain a copy of the License at
+*
+*    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+*
+* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+* limitations under the License.
+*/
+
+'use strict';
+
+// MODULES //
+
+var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
+var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
+var accessors = require( './accessors.js' );
+
+
+// MAIN //
+
+/**
+* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
+*
+* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
+* @param {Collection} x - input array/collection
+* @param {integer} stride - index increment
+* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
+* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
+* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
+* @returns {number} range
+*
+* @example
+* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
+*
+* function accessor( v ) {
+*     return v * 2.0;
+* }
+*
+* var v = rangeBy( x.length, x, 1, 0, accessor );
+* // returns 18.0
+*/
+function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
+var max;
+var min;
+var ix;
+var o;
+var v;
+var i;
+
+if ( N <= 0 ) {
+ NaN;
+}
+o = arraylike2object( x );
+if ( o.accessorProtocol ) {
+ accessors( N, o, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg );
+}
+if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
+= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ offset ], 0, 0, x );
+( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
+ NaN;
+ 0.0;
+}
+ix = offset;
+for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
+= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
+( v !== void 0 ) {
+ = v;
++= stride;
+}
+if ( i === N ) {
+ NaN;
+}
+max = min;
+i += 1;
+for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
++= stride;
+= clbk.call( thisArg, x[ ix ], i, ix, x );
+( v === void 0 ) {
+tinue;
+( isnan( v ) ) {
+ v;
+( v < min ) {
+ = v;
+else if ( v > max ) {
+= v;
+ max - min;
+}
+
+
+// EXPORTS //
+
+module.exports = rangeBy;
+"""
+with open(path, 'w') as f:
+    f.write(content)

+
+'use strict';
+
+// MODULES //
+
+var isnan = require( '@stdlib/math/base/assert/is-nan' );
+
+
+// MAIN //
+
+/**
+* Calculates the range of a strided array via a callback function.
+*
+* @private
+* @param {PositiveInteger} N - number of indexed elements
+* @param {Object} x - input array object
+* @param {Collection} x.data - input array data
+* @param {Array<Function>} x.accessors - array element accessors
+* @param {integer} stride - index increment
+* @param {NonNegativeInteger} offset - starting index
+* @param {Callback} clbk - callback
+* @param {*} [thisArg] - execution context
+* @returns {number} range
+*
+* @example
+* var toAccessorArray = require( '@stdlib/array/base/to-accessor-array' );
+* var arraylike2object = require( '@stdlib/array/base/arraylike2object' );
+*
+* function accessor( v ) {
+*     return v * 2.0;
+* }
+*
+* var x = [ -2.0, 1.0, 3.0, -5.0, 4.0, 0.0, -1.0, -3.0 ];
+* var obj = arraylike2object( toAccessorArray( x ) );
+* var v = rangeBy( x.length, obj, 1, 0, accessor );
+* // returns 18.0
+*/
+function rangeBy( N, x, stride, offset, clbk, thisArg ) {
+var xbuf;
+var get;
+var max;
+var min;
+var ix;
+var v;
+var i;
+
+// Cache reference to array data:
+xbuf = x.data;
+
+// Cache a reference to the element accessor:
+get = x.accessors[ 0 ];
+
+if ( N === 1 || stride === 0 ) {
+= clbk.call( thisArg, get( xbuf, offset ), 0, 0, xbuf );
+( v === void 0 || isnan( v ) ) {
+ NaN;
+ 0.0;
+}
+ix = offset;
+for ( i = 0; i < N; i++ ) {
+= clbk.call( thisArg, get( xbuf, ix ), i, ix, xbuf );
+( v !== void 0 ) {
+ = v;
++= stride;
+}
+if ( i === N ) {
+ NaN;
+}
+max = min;
+i += 1;
+for ( i; i < N; i++ ) {
++= stride;
+= clbk.call( thisArg, get( xbuf, ix ), i, ix, xbuf );
+( v === void 0 ) {
+tinue;
+( isnan( v ) ) {
+ v;
+( v < min ) {
+ = v;
+else if ( v > max ) {
+= v;
+ max - min;
+}
+
+
+// EXPORTS //
+
+module.exports = rangeBy;
